audience: Contractors, developers, architects, site managers and marketing teams planning aerial video for construction or renovation projects
primaryDutchSearchIntent: Bouwbedrijven en projectteams helpen dronevideo doelgericht in te zetten voor marketing, periodieke voortgang en projectcommunicatie zonder operationele registratie met technische inspectie te verwarren

searchIntent.question: How should a construction team plan safe, consistent drone video for progress updates and a finished project case study?
searchIntent.geographicContext: Construction projects in Belgium, subject to current Belgian and European drone rules

longTailKeywords: safe drone filming on a construction site
directAnswer: Drone video works best when each flight records a defined story or comparison point. Use repeatable routes for progress, separate cinematic shots for marketing, coordinate every visit with site management and never treat ordinary marketing footage as a technical inspection or formal site record.
title: Drone video for construction projects: progress, case studies and safe planning
metaDescription: Plan construction drone video with repeatable progress shots, safe site coordination, Belgian flight checks, privacy controls and useful edits for every phase.
h1: Drone video for construction projects: progress, case studies and safe planning
factsToPreserve: Marketing and progress footage is not a technical inspection, survey or formal site report
The guide is not legal advice or approval for a specific flight
Every session requires current site, geozone and safety checks
Open category conditions include the stated 120 metre limit and direct visual line of sight
Site permission does not replace aviation, privacy or location requirements
